Politics.headline: Trump administration retreats on 'Anti-Weaponization Fund'
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump is reportedly backing down from his plan to establish a $1.8 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund” following significant pushback from congressional Republicans; White House officials informed top Republicans on Capitol Hill of the decision on Monday. The Justice Department also indicated it would abide by a court ruling to halt progress after a federal judge temporarily stopped any payouts.

Politics.headline: Iran war day 95: Trump pushes Lebanon truce after Tehran vows to end talks
Politics.detail: Amidst the ongoing conflict, President Trump is advocating for a truce in Lebanon, following Tehran's announcement that it intends to end talks. The situation remains tense as diplomatic efforts continue.

Politics.headline: Celebration, shock and scepticism follow Colombia's presidential election
Politics.detail: Colombia's recent presidential election has elicited a mix of reactions, ranging from celebration and shock to expressions of skepticism. The diverse responses reflect the complex political landscape of the nation.

Politics.headline: Californians head to the polls as governor primary goes down to the wire
Politics.detail: California is holding its primary elections, with the governor's race being closely contested. The outcome of the primary will determine which candidates will advance to the general election.

Politics.headline: Georgia's temporary gas tax suspension will expire at 11:59 tonight
Politics.detail: Georgia's temporary suspension of the gas tax is set to expire at the end of the day. This will likely lead to an increase in gas prices for consumers in the state.

Finance & Economy.headline: Alphabet to sell $80bn in stock to fund AI plans
Finance & Economy.detail: US tech giant Alphabet, Google's parent company, announced a fundraising drive that includes a deal to sell $10bn of stock to Berkshire Hathaway to fund its AI expansion. The company will issue $80 billion in equity to fund AI infrastructure, contrasting with consistent buybacks in recent years.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump signs proclamation amending tariffs on steel, aluminum and copper imports
Finance & Economy.detail: Donald Trump has signed a proclamation amending tariffs on steel, aluminum and copper imports. However, details of the amendments were not available in the search results.

Finance & Economy.headline: Kganyago vows to get inflation rate back to 3%
Finance & Economy.detail: South African Reserve Bank Governor Lesetja Kganyago has pledged to bring the country's inflation rate back to 3%. The governor's commitment comes amid concerns about the impact of the Iran war on the rate outlook.

Finance & Economy.headline: Tribunal finds Bogdanov's false PhD claim was 'intentional and fraudulent'
Finance & Economy.detail: A tribunal has determined that Bogdanov's claim of holding a PhD was intentionally fraudulent. Additional details about the case were not specified in the search results.

Finance & Economy.headline: Chocolate wars: Tiger Brands sells Easter eggs, slabs and Beacon
Finance & Economy.detail: Tiger Brands is actively selling Easter eggs, slabs, and Beacon chocolate products. The report suggests a competitive environment in the chocolate market.

Technology.headline: U.S. big tech holds 85% of Canadian cloud market, report says ahead of AI strategy
Technology.detail: A new report states that Amazon, Microsoft, and Google hold 85% of the public cloud market share in Canada, which is significantly higher than their global average, raising concerns about AI sovereignty as the Canadian government prepares to release its national AI strategy. The report suggests that dependency on U.S. tech giants poses a sovereign risk and competition problem.

Technology.headline: Nvidia Unveils Largest-Ever AI Supercomputer to Power the Agentic AI Era
Technology.detail: Nvidia has launched its largest supercomputing system, the Vera Rubin Nvl 72 server racks, designed for AI data centers, and it's engineered to power AI agents capable of independent reasoning and task execution. The new chip is designed to run AI agents locally, reducing reliance on cloud computing.

Technology.headline: Microsoft's Nvidia-Spark-Powered Surface Laptop Ultra Signals a Raw Power Revolution
Technology.detail: Microsoft's new Surface Laptop Ultra is powered by Nvidia's Spark, signaling a significant leap in processing power. The integration of this superchip promises enhanced AI capabilities and performance for consumer laptops.

Technology.headline: STMicro weighs Crolles fab expansion as AI optics demand rises, CEO says
Technology.detail: STMicroelectronics is considering expanding its Crolles chipmaking site in France by the end of 2026 due to increasing demand for silicon photonics used in AI data centers. The expected shift towards near-package optics, where optical engines are closer to processors, necessitates this potential capacity increase.

Technology.headline: watchTowr Recognized in 2026 Gartner® Emerging Tech: Top Solution Capabilities in Preemptive Cybersecurity
Technology.detail: watchTowr, a leader in Preemptive Exposure Management, has been recognized as an Example Vendor in the 2026 Gartner® Emerging Tech: Top Solution Capabilities in Preemptive Cybersecurity report. Their Preemptive Exposure Management (PEM) platform is designed to help organizations manage and respond to emerging threats in the face of increasing AI-driven cyberattacks.

Science.headline: Astronomers Have Uncovered a Strange Pattern in The Winds of Alien Worlds
Science.detail: A new study reveals a peculiar pattern in the winds of exoplanets, offering insights into their atmospheric dynamics and potentially their habitability.

Science.headline: The World's First Nuclear Waste Tomb Is Nearly Ready to Open
Science.detail: Finland's Onkalo spent nuclear fuel repository, the first of its kind, is nearing completion, designed to safely store nuclear waste for hundreds of thousands of years.

Science.headline: One Common Food Can Fuel Your Workouts And Aid Recovery. Here's The Science.
Science.detail: Scientists have found that a specific readily available food can significantly enhance workout performance and speed up recovery, highlighting its benefits for athletes and fitness enthusiasts.

Science.headline: Physicists Just Achieved 'Perfect Randomness' For The First Time Ever
Science.detail: In a groundbreaking experiment, physicists have achieved perfect randomness, a milestone with significant implications for cryptography, simulations, and fundamental physics research.

Science.headline: Astronomers have finally cracked the mystery behind a strange class of repeating cosmic signals that has baffled scientists for years.
Science.detail: Using Australia's ASKAP radio telescope, researchers traced the origin of repeating fast radio bursts (FRBs), solving a long-standing puzzle in astrophysics.

Health.headline: WVU study shows pregnant women who sit less and move more have fewer adverse outcomes
Health.detail: Research indicates that pregnant women who incorporate light exercise, such as walking, can reduce their risk of adverse pregnancy outcomes like gestational diabetes and preeclampsia, compared to those with prolonged sitting. The study, published in the Journal of the American Medical Association, found a greater risk of adverse outcomes for women sitting 10 or more hours a day.

Health.headline: Can marriage improve your health? Here are the benefits that experts see.
Health.detail: Being married may lower the risk of cardiovascular disease and cancer, while also improving mental health. Research suggests that married people tend to live longer and healthier lives, potentially due to increased social support and better health behaviors.

Health.headline: KFF Health News: Festering Infections to Untreated Cancer: ICE Detainees Describe Medical Neglect Across US
Health.detail: An investigation by KFF Health News and The Associated Press found medical neglect among ICE detainees, ranging from festering infections to untreated cancer. Jill Woodrow reached a tipping point as a caregiver when her mom began struggling to communicate information about her latest doctor appointments.

Health.headline: Health officials oppose US plan to treat Ebola-exposed Americans overseas
Health.detail: U.S. health officials, including former CDC officials, have warned Congress against a proposed policy to treat Americans exposed to Ebola in Kenya or EU countries, citing clinical and ethical concerns. They argue this policy would deviate from medical repatriation practices and could undermine global response efforts.

Health.headline: Medically Tailored Meals Produce Better Health and Lower Costs
Health.detail: A study in Nature Medicine found that Massachusetts Medicaid members receiving medically tailored meals had 31% fewer hospitalizations and 20% fewer emergency department visits. Healthcare costs decreased by $3,433 per person on the meal program, offsetting 98% of the program's cost.

Sports.headline: McLaren admit 'challenging' F1 season is 'below our expectations'
Sports.detail: McLaren boss Andrea Stella has admitted the team have been "below our expectations" after a "challenging" first part of the 2026 Formula 1 season.

Sports.headline: Chelsea confirm signing of Arsenal great McCabe
Sports.detail: Chelsea are set to sign Republic of Ireland captain Katie McCabe when her Arsenal contract expires on July 1.

Sports.headline: Garrett traded to Rams for Verse and 2027 first-round pick
Sports.detail: The Cleveland Browns have agreed terms on a trade to send two-time defensive player of the year Myles Garrett to the Los Angeles Rams.

Sports.headline: Fitzsimmons Foundation $1M Gift To Benefit New Oglebay Sports Center
Sports.detail: A $1 million leadership gift from the Fitzsimmons Foundation will allow a new initiative at Oglebay Park Resort — The Oglebay Sports Center — to hit the ground running. The Oglebay Sports Center's early plans feature a flexible complex featuring indoor and outdoor spaces tailored for a wide range of uses, from youth sports leagues and adult fitness programs to informal recreation and community gatherings.

Sports.headline: WVU pitcher rises to the occasion in elimination game
Sports.detail: Ben McDougal pitched five innings in relief, threw 84 pitches and earned his first victory of the season, as the Mountaineers pulled off an 11-9 come-from-behind victory in front of 4,184 overjoyed fans inside Kendrick Family Ballpark. WVU will now play Kentucky again in a winner-take-all game for the right to advance to the super regional.

Entertainment.headline: Fjord Teaser Trailer: Sebastian Stan, Renate Reinsve's Palme d'Or-Winning Cannes drama unveils new footage
Entertainment.detail: The teaser trailer for 'Fjord,' starring Sebastian Stan and Renate Reinsve, has been released, showcasing new footage from the Palme d'Or-winning Cannes drama.

Entertainment.headline: Mike Myers says 'I'd literally be nothing without you' to Canada in accepting screen award in Toronto
Entertainment.detail: Mike Myers expressed his gratitude to Canada while accepting the Icon Award at the Canadian Screen Awards in Toronto.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Swift Announces Original Song For Toy Story 5
Entertainment.detail: Taylor Swift is releasing a new original song, "I Knew It I Knew You," for the Toy Story 5 soundtrack, which she wrote after seeing the film in its early stages; the song will be released on Friday before the movie hits theaters on June 19th.

Entertainment.headline: Horror films by YouTubers win big at box office
Entertainment.detail: Two small-budget horror films made by YouTube creators, "Backrooms" and "Obsession," had a big weekend at the box office, signaling a shift in the movie industry.

Entertainment.headline: CORUS ENTERTAINMENT EXTENDS GLOBAL'S THE MORNING SHOW WITH THE LAUNCH OF TMS2, A NEW AFTERNOON LIFESTYLE PROGRAM HOSTED BY MORGAN HOFFMAN DEBUTING THIS FALL
Entertainment.detail: Global's The Morning Show is expanding with TMS2, a new afternoon lifestyle program hosted by Morgan Hoffman, which will air weekdays at 1 p.m. on Global and stream on STACKTV starting this September, featuring celebrity interviews and expert-driven segments.

Crime & Justice.headline: Police condemn calls for 'vigilante justice' over murder of Henry Nowak
Crime & Justice.detail: Police officers have condemned calls for “vigilante justice” against their colleagues over the handling of the murder of Henry Nowak, who was falsely accused of racism by a Sikh man who had fatally stabbed him; the police watchdog is investigating.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man to serve more than 11 years for sex crimes conviction
Crime & Justice.detail: A Port Ludlow man has been sentenced to more than 11 years in prison following his conviction on two counts of first-degree child molestation; the mother documented portions of the incident on video.

Crime & Justice.headline: Trial continues as Gaza protesters defend Golden Gate Bridge blockade
Crime & Justice.detail: The trial of seven protesters arrested on the Golden Gate Bridge in 2024 for protesting Israel's actions in Gaza will resume, with the defendants facing felony conspiracy and misdemeanor charges and a maximum of 14-15 years in prison if convicted.

Crime & Justice.headline: Who, Where, How and Why? A data-driven look behind migrant smuggling around the world.
Crime & Justice.detail: The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime released a data-driven analysis of migrant smuggling around the world.
Crime & Justice.source: United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime

Crime & Justice.headline: High Courts Expected To Record Nature Of Crime And Allegations In FIR Quashing Orders
Crime & Justice.detail: The Supreme Court has advised High Courts to mention the contents of the FIR and the nature of allegations in quashing orders, to help the Supreme Court understand the application of mind at the High Court level.

Education.headline: Home Office issues amendment after The PIE reports on BCA blind spot
Education.detail: The Home Office has published an amendment to the student sponsor compliance policy, clarifying that only visa refusal decisions that are successfully overturned by administrative review will be exempt from BCA calculations.

Education.headline: Barney Glover appointed inaugural chief commissioner of Australia's tertiary watchdog
Education.detail: Australia's new tertiary education watchdog is beginning to take shape, with former university leader Barney Glover appointed inaugural chief commissioner, alongside three statutory commissioners tasked with driving major sector reforms.

Education.headline: New UKVI compliance metrics for universities take effect today
Education.detail: New compliance metrics from the UK Visas and Immigration (UKVI) for universities have taken effect today.

Education.headline: Third of people no longer believe degree is worth the time or money, UK poll shows
Education.detail: A recent UK poll reveals that a third of people no longer believe a university degree is worth the time or money.

Education.headline: University of Manchester to investigate sexual harassment of female medical students
Education.detail: The University of Manchester will conduct an investigation into allegations of sexual harassment against female medical students.

Lifestyle.headline: Neurologist shares simple '9-year longevity equation' for healthier ageing
Lifestyle.detail: A neurologist has shared habits involving sleep, exercise, and nutrition that contribute to healthy aging and potentially add nine years to one's life.

Lifestyle.headline: Alia Bhatt shares her 'beauty essential': 'Saw my score was above 85'
Lifestyle.detail: Bollywood actress Alia Bhatt revealed her go-to beauty product and shared that she received a high score after using it.

Lifestyle.headline: Bobby Deol works out six days a week: 'Didn't want a bodybuilder look'
Lifestyle.detail: Actor Bobby Deol discusses his fitness regimen and his desire to achieve a balanced physique rather than a bulky, bodybuilder appearance.

Lifestyle.headline: BTS to release special vinyl edition of Arirang album with bonus tracks ahead of 13th year anniversary
Lifestyle.detail: The album will feature all tracks of the latest album, Arirang, as well as two bonus songs.

Lifestyle.headline: I bullied a barber into cutting my fringe. It was a terrible mistake.
Lifestyle.detail: Zoe Williams reflects on a recent haircutting experience gone wrong.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Russia's cruel way of war
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The deliberate brutality of Vladimir Putin's war in Ukraine has a long history.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Collection of outstanding debts owed Baltimore needs attention
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The city has a $64 million budget deficit and a recent audit by city government showed that the city had neglected to collect $4 million in fines and penalty revenue related to parking and other traffic matters, and with late penalties, they now exceed $280 million.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Dead Last: West Virginia lawmakers ignoring economic realities
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Total employment declined by 6,700 jobs from March 2025 to March 2026, with noteworthy downturns in the mining, manufacturing, and leisure/hospitality industries.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: That wasn't a trend. It was representation
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The voters of the 31st District repeatedly chose a Democrat who did not fit neatly into ideological categories because they valued thoughtful representation over partisan loyalty.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: What Education Reporting Gets Right—and Wrong (Opinion)
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Education is predominantly a local and state issue, and it's those newsrooms that have been most decimated by cuts, and many reporters at local outlets are stretched so thin that they don't have sufficient time to dig deeply into topics, find sources, launch investigations, or thoroughly cover all the daily news.

World.headline: U.S. Shoots Down Iranian Missiles Targeting American Military in Kuwait
World.detail: Iran fired ballistic missiles at American troops in Kuwait early Monday morning, and U.S. forces shot them down, marking the latest flare-up in a shaky ceasefire. Over the weekend, the U.S. bombed Iranian radar and drone sites after Iran shot down an American drone.

World.headline: Trump Says Israel and Hezbollah Have Agreed to Dial Back Fighting
World.detail: President Trump stated that both Israel and Hezbollah have agreed to de-escalate their conflict after he spoke with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu and communicated with Hezbollah through mediators.

World.headline: Protests Erupt in Kenya Objecting to Proposed Ebola Treatment Center
World.detail: Protests have erupted in Kenya over a proposed Ebola treatment center, with demonstrators demanding it be removed from the country, while a Kenyan court has frozen the plan amid legal challenges.

World.headline: Russia Launches Major Attack Across Ukraine, Killing at Least 18
World.detail: Russian air attacks on major Ukrainian cities, including Kyiv and Dnipro, have resulted in at least 18 deaths and numerous injuries, following warnings of a planned major assault.

World.headline: Han Zheng Meets Visiting UK Foreign Secretary
World.detail: Chinese Vice President Han Zheng met with UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper, calling for both countries to work together to promote multilateralism and stability, while also discussing trade, AI, and climate change.

Zimbabwe.headline: CARE Zimbabwe appoints first local country director in 34 years
Zimbabwe.detail: Delilah Takawira takes over the leadership role, becoming the first local national to head CARE Zimbabwe in its 34-year history, bringing over two decades of experience in development, humanitarian, and public-sector work. Her appointment reflects the organization's commitment to strengthening locally led development and humanitarian action.

Zimbabwe.headline: Government extends winter wheat planting deadline to June 15
Zimbabwe.detail: The government has extended the winter wheat planting deadline to June 15, aiming to ensure sufficient wheat production. This extension provides farmers with additional time to complete planting and potentially increase the overall yield for the season.

Zimbabwe.headline: Battery manufacturers warn imports threaten 500 jobs
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe's battery manufacturing industry is cautioning that more than 500 jobs and a critical industrial sector are at risk due to imports. The industry is urging measures to protect local manufacturers from being undercut by cheaper imported batteries.

Zimbabwe.headline: Brickstone directors acquitted in US$1.5 million Harare land scandal case
Zimbabwe.detail: Directors from Brickstone were acquitted in a US$1.5 million Harare land scandal case. The court found insufficient evidence to convict them on charges related to the land deal.

Zimbabwe.headline: Climate change now a governance, economic issue – Govt, ActionAid
Zimbabwe.detail: The government and ActionAid have recognized climate change as a significant governance and economic issue. This acknowledgment signals a shift towards integrating climate considerations into broader policy and economic frameworks.
